Attack +3; Reach 5 ft.; Targets one;

Graze [[damage 1d4 Keen average]];

Hit [[damage 1d4 +3 Keen]]

Attack +4; Range 150/600 ft; Targets one;

Graze [[damage 1d6 Keen average]];

Hit [[damage 1d6 + 4 Keen average]]
On the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name}'s first turn of each scene, if they aren't Surprised, they can use the Gain Advantage action as 0.

When an enemy the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} can sense moves while the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} is within 150 feet of them, the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} makes a Longbow attack against them. On a hit, the target is also Immobilized until the end of the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name}'s next turn.

At the start of each scene, if the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} is in cover or an area where their enemy’s primary sense is obscured, the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} can make a [[test skill=stl]] (no action required) with an advantage against the Spiritual defense of each enemy who can sense them. Each enemy they succeed against loses track of them and can no longer sense them until the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} attacks or takes another action that would expose them.
While the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} is in cover or an area where their enemy's primary sense is obscured, their Physical defense increases by 2 (to 18) against that enemy.

Attack +8; Reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft.; Targets one;

Graze [[damage 1d4 Keen average]];

Hit [[damage 1d4 + 8 Keen average]], and if the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} had at least one advantage on this attack, they can spend 1 focus to deal an extra [[damage 4d4 Keen average]].

Attack +8; Range 80/320 ft; Targets one;

Graze [[damage 1d6 Keen average]];

Hit [[damage 1d6 + 8 Keen average]], and the target must succeed on a [[test skill=ath dc=13]] or be Slowed until the end of the target's next turn.

After the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} ends a Move action within 5 feet of an enemy, if the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} moved at least 20 feet this turn, they can use the Gain Advantage action as 0.
Once per turn, when the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} grazes, they can add their attack test modifier to that graze's damage.
The [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} gains an advantage on non-attack tests that rely on smell.
The [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name}'s attacks can't critically hit, and they're immediately defeated when they suffer an injury.

Attack +4; Reach 5 ft.; Targets one;

Graze [[damage 1d4 Keen average]];

Hit [[damage 1d4 + 4 Keen average]], and if the target is Medium or smaller, the [[lookup @actor.name]] can spend 1 focus to knock the target Prone, then move up to 10 feet while dragging the target behind them.
While within 5 feet of an ally, the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} can use the Gain Advantage action as 0.
After an enemy within 30 feet of the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} falls Prone, the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} moves up to 15 feet toward them.
The [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name}'s attacks can't critically hit, and they're immediately defeated when they suffer an injury.

If the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} moves at least 10 feet in a straight line toward a target then makes a Mace attack against them on that turn, the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} gains an advantage on that attack.

The [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} makes an [[test skill=ath]] against a target within 5 feet of them, opposed by the target's choice of Athletics or Agility. If the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} succeeds on this test, the target is knocked Prone.

Attack +2; Reach 5 ft.; Targets one;

Graze [[damage 1d6 Impact average]];

Hit [[damage 1d6 + 2 Impact average]]

Attack +2; Range 80/120 ft; Targets one;

Graze [[damage 1d6 Keen average]];

Hit [[damage 1d6 + 2 Keen average]]
The [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} can only use the Move action once per turn.

Attack +6; Reach 10 ft.; Targets one;

Graze [[damage 1d6 Keen average]];

Hit [[damage 1d6 + 6 Keen average]]

The [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} retracts their head and legs to defend themself until the start of their next turn. While retracted, the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name}'s deflect value increases to 6 and they can use the Brace action as if they were in cover. At the start of each of their turns, the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} can spend 1 focus to maintain this effect for an additional round.
The [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name}'s carrying capacity is 1,500 lbs.
The [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name}'s attacks can't critically hit, and they're immediately defeated when they suffer an injury.
The [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} has a utility expertise in one profession, and they gain an advantage on skill tests related to that profession.

Attack +1; Reach 5 ft.; Targets one;

Graze [[damage 1d4 Impact average]];

Hit [[damage 1d4 + 1 Impact average]]

On a Complication, this improvised weapon breaks.
When an ally makes a test against an enemy who is within reach of the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name}, the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} grants the ally an advantage on that test.

The swarm is compromised of many tiny creatures, and it can't regain health. It can move through spaces occupied by other characters or through openings with a diameter of 3 inches, as long as it ends its movement fully on the other side of the character or obstacle.

Attack +5; Reach 5 ft.; Targets one;

The [[lookup @actor.name]] has more than half its health.

Graze [[damage 2d4 Keen average]];

Hit [[damage 2d4 + 5 Keen average]]

Attack +5; Reach 5 ft.; Targets one;

The [[lookup @actor.name]] has half its health or less.

Graze [[damage 1d4 Keen average]];

Hit [[damage 1d4 + 2 Keen average]]

The swarm emits an ear-piercing cacophony of screeching and clicking. Each character within 10 feet of the swarm must make a [[test skill=dis dc=14]]. On a failure, a character must spend 2 focus or become Disoriented until the end of their next turn. Cremlings and characters who can't hear automatically succeed on this test.
The [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} has a utility expertise in one profession, and they gain an advantage on skill tests related to that profession.
After the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} spends focus to resist a character's influence, that character loses 1 focus.

Attack +2; Reach 5 ft.; Targets one;

Graze [[damage 1d4 Impact average]];

Hit [[damage 1d4 + 2 Impact average]]
During a conversation, the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} makes a [[test skill=prs defence=spi]]. On a success, the target loses 3 focus.

If the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} isn't at full Investiture, they choose one of the following targets within 10 feet of them, draining the chosen target of power:

  • Object with Charges. A fabrial or other object with charges loses [[/r 1d6]] charges.

  • Group of Spheres. Up to [[/r 1d6]] infused marks or broams within range lose the Stormlight or other Light infused in them.

  • Infused Object or Character. The target (or effect infused in the target) loses [[/r 1d6]] Investiture.

If the target is a character, or if it is an object worn or held by a character, the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} must succeed on an Agility test against the character's Spiritual defense or the target isn't drained.

The [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} regains the same amount of Investiture as they drained from the target. A character wearing Invested Shardplate can't have their Investiture drained in this way, but the Shardplate itself can be drained of charges.

The [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} becomes Enhanced [Strength +1] and Enhanced [Speed +1] until the end of their next turn. This temporarily increases each of their Physical skills by 1, including their Bite action's attack and damage rolls. At the end of each of the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name}'s turns, they can spend 1 Investiture to maintain this effect for an additional round.
The [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} can spend 1 Investiture to recover [[damage 1d6 + 4 Healing average]].
The [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} doesn't trigger Reactive Strikes while flying.

Attack +8; Reach 5 ft.; Targets one;

Graze [[damage 2d6 Keen average]];

Hit [[damage 2d6 + 8 Keen average]], and the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} can spend 1 focus to use their Drain Light (no action required) on that target or an effect infusing them. The [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} automatically succeeds on their Agility test to do so.

cultural:languages.label: Languages: understands every Rosharan language, and can speak short, simple sentences

system.notes:
Special Companion: Larkin

Larkin are critically endangered creatures who were associated with the Knights Radiant before the Era of Solitude. These lithe crustaceans have narrow faces, eyes of pure silver, and wings that fold from their backs. Their most defining characteristic, though, is their ability to feed on Investiture: a larkin can drain Light from gems, fabrials, and even living beings (like Radiants and Fused). It is speculated that they may be able to consume other forms of Investiture as well.

Though larkin are able to subsist for a short time on more standard foodstuffs, they become weak and sickly if they don’t consume Light for an extended period.

Modern larkin are diminutive, generally between the length of an adult hand and forearm. However, once a larkin consumes enough Investiture in their lifetime, they can be returned to their ancestral homeland to attain the next stage of growth. This allows them to grow larger and stronger, and scholars believe that a larkin may be able to do this several times, though this hasn’t been proven.

Gaining a Larkin

A larkin can be given as a reward for a character of any tier. However, due to their extreme rarity, it’s highly unlikely that more than one character in a campaign could obtain a larkin companion.

Growing Your Larkin

If you are tier 3 or higher and you have a larkin companion, the GM can offer you the goal “Grow Your Larkin.” To complete this goal, you must brave the dangerous journey to the ruins of Aimia with your larkin, allow them to bond with a special type of luckspren, and return alive. The tier 3 reward for this is your larkin molting into a greater larkin.

If the target is a character, or if it is an object worn or held by a character, the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} must succeed on an Agility test against the character's Spiritual defense or the target isn't drained.

At the end of the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name}'s turn, if they're in cover or an area where their enemy’s primary sense is obscured, the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} can make a Stealth test (no action required) with an advantage against the Spiritual defense of each enemy who can sense them. On a hit or graze, if the target is Disoriented, they take an extra [[damage 1d4 Keen average]].

The [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} throws sand, flour, or a similar substance to distract and confuse foes. Each enemy within 10 feet of them must succeed on a [[test skill=agi dc=13]] or become Disoriented until the end of the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name}'s next turn.

The [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} attempts to rifle through the belongings of a humanoid within 5 feet of them, making an opposed [[test skill=thv]] against the target's Perception. If the target is Disoriented, the target gains a disadvantage on their test. If the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} succeeds, they can choose to steal up to 50 marks or one item the target isn't wielding or wearing.

After an enemy ends a Move action within 5 feet of the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name}, the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} can use the Disengage action as r. If the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} ends this movement in cover or in an area where an enemy's primary sense is obscured, enemies affected by that cover or obscured senses gain a disadvantage on attacks against the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name} until the end of the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name}'s next turn.

When an enemy rolls a c on an attack test against the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name}, the enemy gains a +3 bonus to that d20 roll. Regardless of whether the attack hits or misses, if any other characters are within 5 feet of the [[lookup @actor.name]]{Actor Name}, the enemy must choose one. The enemy's attack also grazes the chosen character (without spending focus).
